content.full_text:
August 7, 1998


Office of County Counsel
William Murano
Lassen County Counsel
221 South Roop Street, Suite 2
Susanville, CA 96130

Re:    Your Request For Advice
       Our File No. I-98-030(a)

Dear Mr. Murano:

       This is in response to your letter of May 4, 1998, on behalf of County Public Defender
Thomas Anderson, requesting reconsideration of our previous advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

       In our February 23, 1998, letter to Mr. Peter Bianchi, Deputy Public Defender (copy
enclosed), we confirmed the written advice that the Commission recommends the position of
Deputy Public Defender be included in the Lassen County Conflict of Interest Code. (Nagel
Advice Letter, No. A-97-466, and Bianchi Advice Letter, No. I-98-030.) You have submitted
additional information that may modify those facts set forth in our initial advice. However, the
subsequent facts do not alter our original conclusion.

       As previously stated, the Commission can only offer advice regarding which employees
should be designated under a county's conflict of interest code. (Section 87312.) Although we
recommended that the Deputy Public Defenders be included in Lassen County's Conflict of
Interest Code, this was a recommendation only, and not binding on the County or on the Board
of Supervisors as the County's code reviewing body. The Board of Supervisors alone has the
authority to determine what facts are used to reach their final determination.




_________________________

       ¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-98-030(a)
Page 2

Therefore, any facts that may support an amendment to the Lassen County Conflict of
Interest Code should be addressed to the County Board of Supervisors.

If you have any questions on this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel


By:     Margaret E. Figeroid
        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

However, the Commission clarifies that its recommendation is advisory only, and the Board of Supervisors alone has the authority to determine what facts support any amendment to the county's conflict of interest code.

Letter references previous advice letters (Nagel A-97-466 and Bianchi 1-98-030). This is a reconsideration/follow-up advice letter with moderately high confidence in extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should the position of Deputy Public Defender be included in Lassen County's Conflict of Interest Code, and does additional information submitted in a reconsideration request alter the Commission's previous advice on this matter?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC reaffirms its previous recommendation that Deputy Public Defenders be included in Lassen County's Conflict of Interest Code. However, the Commission clarifies that its recommendation is advisory only, and the Board of Supervisors alone has the authority to determine what facts support any amendment to the county's conflict of interest code.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC response to a reconsideration request regarding whether Deputy Public Defenders should be included in Lassen County's Conflict of Interest Code. The Commission reaffirms its previous recommendation but clarifies that such recommendations are advisory and the final determination rests with the Board of Supervisors.
